channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Brown lakes shift fish populations as trout and bass decline
item.description: Freshwater lakes in North America and Europe are turning browner, reducing visibility and altering fish communities, with pike and walleye thriving.

item.title: Neurons must break their DNA to build the brain, scientists discover
item.description: Newborn neurons squeeze through tight spaces in the developing brain, and research shows they must break their DNA to reach their destinations.

item.title: Scientists say human anatomy still holds undiscovered secrets
item.description: After centuries of study, researchers continue to find new details and overlooked structures in the human body.

item.title: Early primates may have evolved in cold climates, not tropics
item.description: A new study suggests the earliest primates originated in cold, dry parts of North America rather than tropical forests.
